supported_goal: Help the customer check the delivery status and expected arrival of a physical Northwind card that has already been issued and mailed.
included: physical debit or credit card in the mail
mailing address the card was sent to
excluded: activating the card once it arrives
reporting a lost or stolen card and its replacement
key_facts: Newly issued cards ship by standard mail and arrive in 5-7 business days; track status in the app under Cards > Track delivery.
Expedited shipping is available for $25 and delivers in 2 business days.
Cards are mailed to the address on file; if it has not arrived after 10 business days, request a replacement via the 24/7 line.

supported_goal: Guide the customer through changing the 4-digit PIN on their Northwind debit or credit card.
key_facts: Change your 4-digit card PIN in the app under Cards > Manage > Change PIN, or at any Northwind ATM.
The new PIN takes effect immediately and there is no fee.
The card PIN is separate from your mobile-app login password and from any OTP code.

required_discriminators.clarifying_question: Do you mean the PIN for your Northwind debit or credit card, or a different PIN (SIM, voicemail, app)?
required_discriminators.ambiguous_because: a bare 'PIN' commonly means a SIM, voicemail, or app password, which are out of scope

supported_goal: Show the customer their current credit-card statement balance and when that balance is due.
excluded: making the payment / transferring funds to pay it
key_facts: View your statement balance and due date in the app under Accounts > Credit Card > Statement.
The statement closes monthly on your closing date and payment is due 21 days later (grace period).
The minimum payment is the greater of 2% of the statement balance or $25.

required_discriminators.clarifying_question: Do you mean your credit-card statement balance / amount due, or something else (checking/savings balance, available credit)?
required_discriminators.ambiguous_because: a bare 'balance' commonly means a checking/savings account balance, which no card covers (out of scope)

supported_goal: Explain the foreign-exchange fee Northwind charges when the card is used for international or foreign-currency transactions.
key_facts: Northwind charges a 3% foreign transaction fee on the USD amount of any international card purchase or foreign-currency transaction.
The fee is waived on Northwind Global Checking premium accounts.
This FX fee is separate from any out-of-network ATM fee.

supported_goal: Help the customer report a lost or stolen Northwind card, freeze it, and get a replacement.
excluded: a card that was merely declined at a merchant
key_facts: Report a lost or stolen card 24/7 via the app (Cards > Report lost/stolen) or the 1-800-NORTHWIND hotline; the card is frozen immediately.
A free replacement arrives in 5-7 business days, or in 2 business days for $25 expedited.
Stolen cards are reissued with a new card number; a merely misplaced card can instead be temporarily frozen and unfrozen in the app.

supported_goal: Help the customer move money between their own Northwind accounts (e.g., checking to savings).
excluded: paying a credit card bill from an outside account
key_facts: Transfers between your own Northwind accounts are instant and free via Transfer > Between my accounts.
There is no transfer fee and no dollar cap beyond your available balance.
Transfers to other banks (ACH) or to other people are a separate service and are not covered here.

in_scope_values: between the customer's own Northwind accounts (checking <-> savings)
required_discriminators.clarifying_question: Do you mean a transfer between your own Northwind accounts, or to another person / another bank?
required_discriminators.ambiguous_because: a bare 'transfer' commonly means sending money to another person or an external bank, which is out of scope

supported_goal: Explain why a specific card transaction was declined at a merchant and what the customer can do about it.
excluded: reporting the card lost or stolen
disputing a charge that did go through
key_facts: Common decline reasons are insufficient funds, exceeding the daily spending limit, an unactivated or expired card, an incorrect PIN, or a fraud/travel block.
The specific reason appears in the app's transaction notifications; resolve it by adjusting the limit, activating the card, or setting a travel notice.
A declined card is not the same as a lost/stolen card and does not need to be reported unless you suspect fraud.

supported_goal: Guide the customer through resetting the login password for the Northwind mobile app.
key_facts: Reset your mobile-app login password from the login screen via "Forgot password."
Verify your identity with a one-time passcode sent to your registered phone or email, then set a new password.
This is the app login password, not your 4-digit card PIN.

required_discriminators.clarifying_question: Do you mean your Northwind mobile-app login password, or something else (username recovery, card PIN)?
required_discriminators.ambiguous_because: a bare 'reset it' can mean a username, card PIN, or third-party password, which are out of scope

supported_goal: Guide the customer through activating a newly received Northwind card so it can be used.
excluded: tracking a card still in the mail
adding the card to a mobile wallet
key_facts: Activate a newly received card in the app (Cards > Activate), by calling 1-800-NORTHWIND, or at any Northwind ATM.
Activate within 30 days of receiving the card.
The card must be activated before it can be used or added to a mobile wallet.

supported_goal: Help the customer find a nearby in-network, surcharge-free ATM.
included: ATM near me / by ZIP code
key_facts: Find surcharge-free in-network ATMs in the app under Locations > ATM finder, using your current location or a ZIP code.
Northwind and Allpoint together offer 55,000+ surcharge-free ATMs.
Using an out-of-network ATM incurs a $3 Northwind fee.

supported_goal: Help the customer dispute an unauthorized or incorrect charge that has already POSTED to their account.
excluded: reporting the card lost or stolen
key_facts: Dispute an unauthorized or incorrect POSTED charge in the app (open the transaction > Dispute) or by phone; only posted, not pending, charges can be disputed.
File within 60 days of the statement that shows the charge.
Provisional credit is issued within 10 business days and the investigation can take up to 90 days.

supported_goal: Help the customer view or change the daily purchase limit on their card.
excluded: why a specific purchase was declined
key_facts: View or change your card's daily purchase limit in the app under Cards > Spending limit.
The default daily purchase limit is $2,000 and can be set anywhere from $0 to $5,000; changes apply immediately.
The daily ATM cash-withdrawal limit is a separate setting (default $500).

required_discriminators.clarifying_question: Do you mean your daily card spending (purchase) limit, or the ATM withdrawal limit / credit-line increase?
required_discriminators.ambiguous_because: a bare 'limit' commonly means the ATM withdrawal limit or a credit-line increase, which are out of scope

supported_goal: Help the customer download a monthly account statement as a PDF file.
excluded: disputing a transaction on the statement
key_facts: Download a monthly statement as a PDF in the app under Accounts > Statements > Download PDF, or via online banking.
Statements are available for the past 84 months (7 years) and there is no fee.
This delivers the PDF document, which is different from viewing your current statement balance.

supported_goal: Guide the customer through adding their Northwind card to Apple Pay or Google Pay.
excluded: adding the card to a merchant app
key_facts: Add your card to Apple Pay or Google Pay from the app (Cards > Add to Apple Pay / Google Pay) or directly in the wallet app.
The physical card must be activated first.
The wallet stores a tokenized card number and no additional fee applies.

supported_goal: Help the customer set a travel notice so their cards keep working abroad without being blocked for suspected fraud.
excluded: why a card was declined after travel started
key_facts: Set a travel notice in the app under Cards > Travel notice, entering your destinations and travel dates.
Set it at least 3 days before you travel to prevent the card from being blocked abroad for suspected fraud.
A travel notice does not waive the 3% foreign transaction fee.

supported_goal: Help the customer update the phone number or email address on their Northwind account.
key_facts: Update your phone number or email in the app under Profile > Contact info.
Email changes take effect immediately; phone-number changes require a one-time passcode to verify.
Mailing-address and name changes are handled separately, not through this flow.

required_discriminators.clarifying_question: Do you mean your phone number or email, or something else (mailing address, name)?
required_discriminators.ambiguous_because: a bare 'update my info / details' can mean a home-address or name change, which are out of scope
